name: Aluminum
summary: Day 76. **B/C3 LME ALUMINUM $3,624.50/mt (-5.6% 1D, +0.3% 1M, +22.0% 3M) — BIGGEST 1D DROP IN COMMODITY COMPLEX TODAY.** Drivers (~50% crisis, ~50% macro/demand): Position unwind + Discovery Alert '$3,559/t May 8 → $3,624 May 14' suggests cycle-low rebound got monetised; demand caution (Q2 industrial output softening) + USD bid. Gulf-supply premium UNWIND on Trump-Xi 'trade-not-war' opening + crisis-trade unwind across complex (Lockheed -15% MoM, CF -3.8%, Cheniere -2.0%, RBOB -4.5%, Frontline -3.5%). Per markets dossier B: 'positions long China-reflation (Cu +2.5%/Ag +2.6% prior session), short war-premium-supply (Al)' — today both retraced. **Copper $6.58/lb (-0.9% 1D, +8.3% 1M)** = trade-not-war narrative bid earlier retraced today; gold/silver/copper all mildly weaker = paper-physical bifurcation in metals complex reads as 'partial de-escalation pricing.' Gulf production damage unresolved (Borouge UAE, ALBA Bahrain, EGA Al Taweelah) but already in price. **Assessment: aluminum cleanest counter-signal to crisis premium re-rating — if war is truly tightening industrial-commodity supply broadly, aluminum should rally with copper. Instead BOTH retracing on Trump-Xi summit-pricing.** PRIOR (May 13): Aluminum had run to ~$3,600/mt 4-year high; current $3,510 = -2.5% from peak. **CANCELLED-WARRANT DECLINE -7% TO 26,500t SIGNALS REDUCED NEAR-TERM PHYSICAL DEMAND** — inventory queue shrinking, not deepening. Persian Gulf ~9% of global aluminum output — meaningful but not dominant; Gulf supply concerns ALREADY priced in +44% YoY level. **ASSESSMENT (markets dossier C8): Aluminum is cleanest counter-signal to fertilizer rally. If war were truly tightening industrial-commodity supply broadly, aluminum should rally with copper (+8.1% 1M, +11.9% 3M). Instead, aluminum showing DEMAND-SIDE SOFTNESS — likely China property + global industrial moderation.** **This is a signal that NOT ALL CRISIS-LINKED MOVES ARE CRISIS-DRIVEN; the global industrial cycle has its own tempo.** Watch for confirmation: if copper rolls over while gold stays muted, inflation/flow regime is shifting toward outright demand-softness regime — bad for cyclicals across the board. China May trade-data tape weakness (USD/CNY 6.79, Shanghai Composite -0.4%, property-sector weakness) contributes. Gulf production damage unresolved (Borouge UAE, ALBA Bahrain, EGA Al Taweelah) but already in price; ~10% crisis-residual, ~90% non-crisis demand-softness. Per markets.md D6: aluminum down -2.7% Apr 29 on profit-taking after April rally; Brent +6-8% to $118-120 should normally support Gulf-aluminum-supply-disruption thesis but profit-taking dominated. LME inventories contracted 0.58% to 381,050 tonnes — supply tight. Trade-policy normalization expectations. Gulf production damage unresolved: Borouge (UAE) shut since Apr 5; ALBA (Bahrain) 19% offline; EGA (UAE) Al Taweelah damage unrepaired. ~9% of global aluminum capacity at risk; physical-damage repair/restart 12-18 months structural. Trump-Axios 'choking like a stuffed pig' rejection of Hormuz-First reinforces structural thesis but day's tape was positioning-driven, not fundamentals. PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY (preserved): INFO-OPS-ADJACENT DIVERGENCE FLAGGED: Monday attribution to 'Iranian authorities stated that commercial vessels are now free to navigate the chokepoint following ongoing talks with the US' (Trading Economics/Mining.com) is OPPOSITE of the OPERATIONAL reality — 16 vessels Monday vs 138 baseline = 88% suppression; Sunday zero-transit. Someone trading aluminum read the soft Iran DIPLOMATIC quote and ignored AIS data. Also profit-taking after +11.5% 1M and $3,670 four-year high earlier in April + China export-increase expectations. Aluminum now CHEAP relative to operational reality if Wednesday ceasefire lapses — +$200-300/mt catch-down in 48-72hr. Gulf production damage unresolved: Borouge (UAE) shut since April 5; ALBA (Bahrain) 19% offline; EGA (UAE) Al Taweelah damage unrepaired. ~9% of global aluminum capacity at risk. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]

historical_summaries.summary: 2 signals. IRGC claimed attacks on ALBA (Bahrain) and EGA (UAE) aluminum facilities in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes on Iranian steel plants | LME aluminum surged to $3,492-$3,500/mt — four-year high — following the ALBA/EGA attacks
5 signals. Aluminum LME at $3,422/mt (-0 | Gulf aluminum exposure unchanged: ALBA (Bahrain) and EGA (UAE) remain in the cumulative damage envelope | Day 50
